project.name: DocSpec Processor (Java)
project.description: Annotation processor with auto-discovery for generating docspec.json

performance.bottleneck: DSTI intent extraction across all methods when Trees API is available

members.name: AbstractTreesChannel
members.description:
Base class for channels that need AST (Trees) access.
 Provides helper methods for reflective Tree API access.

members.name: AssignmentChainChannel
members.description:
Channel 9: Constants (Assignment Chain).
 Scans for UPPER_CASE constant references in the method body.
 Falls back to detecting static final fields in the enclosing class when Trees is unavailable.
 Sets the constants list on signals.

members.name: AssignmentPatternChannel
members.description:
Channel 6: Assignment Patterns (Data Flow).
 Traces data flow in the method body: builder patterns, constructor calls,
 and assignment chains. Populates the data flow writes list.
 Falls back to parameter type analysis when Trees is unavailable.

members.name: BranchStructureChannel
members.description:
Channel 3: Branch Structure.
 Counts if-statements (branches) from the Trees AST when available.
 Falls back to heuristic branch estimation from method signature when Trees is unavailable:
 methods returning boolean or Optional, or having multiple parameters, likely contain branches.

members.name: ConstantChannel
members.description:
Channel 11: Assertions (Constant).
 Detects assert statements from the AST and assertion-style method calls
 ({@code check*}, {@code require*}, {@code verify*}) from the method source.
 Falls back to detecting assertion-related annotations when Trees is unavailable.
 Sets the assertions count on signals.

members.name: EqualityChannel
members.description:
Channel 13: Validation Annotations (Equality).
 Counts validation annotations on method parameters ({@code @NotNull}, {@code @Valid},
 {@code @Min}, {@code @Max}, {@code @Size}, etc.).
 Sets the validationAnnotations count on signals.

members.name: ErrorHandlingChannel
members.description:
Channel 8: Error Handling.
 Counts try/catch blocks and extracts caught exception types from Trees AST.
 Falls back to declared thrown types when Trees is unavailable.
 Sets the errorHandling model on signals.

members.name: ExceptionMessageChannel
members.description:
Channel 10: Null Checks (Exception Messages).
 Counts null check patterns ({@code != null}, {@code == null},
 {@code Objects.requireNonNull}, {@code Optional.ofNullable}, {@code Optional.of}) from source.
 Falls back to counting @NonNull/@Nullable annotations on parameters when Trees is unavailable.
 Sets the nullChecks count on signals.

members.name: GuardClauseChannel
members.description:
Channel 2: Guard Clauses.
 Counts if-throw patterns in the method body as guard clauses.
 Also counts {@code @DocIntentional} and {@code @DocPreserves} annotation-based guards.
 Requires Trees for body analysis, but has an annotation-based fallback.

members.name: IntentChannel
members.description:
Interface for individual DSTI intent extraction channels.
 Each channel analyzes one specific aspect of method behavior.
 Channels are independent and can be enabled/disabled for ablation studies.

methods.name: channelNumber
methods.description: Channel number (1-13) per the DSTI specification.

methods.name: requiresTrees
methods.description: Whether this channel requires the com.sun.source.util.Trees API for AST analysis.

methods.name: extract
methods.description: Extract intent signals from the given method and merge them into the signals model.

params.name: trees
params.description: the Trees instance (may be null if requiresTrees() returns false)

params.name: methodTree
params.description: the method's tree representation from Trees.getTree() (may be null)

params.name: signals
params.description: the signals model to populate (merge into, do not overwrite other channels)

members.name: LoggingChannel
members.description:
Channel 12: Logging.
 Detects logger method calls ({@code log}, {@code debug}, {@code info},
 {@code warn}, {@code error}, {@code trace}) from method source.
 Falls back to detecting Logger-typed fields in the enclosing class when Trees is unavailable.
 Sets the logStatements count on signals.

members.name: LoopPatternChannel
members.description:
Channel 7: Loop Patterns.
 Detects {@code .stream()}, {@code Stream.}, enhanced-for loops from Trees AST.
 Falls back to parameter/return type analysis when Trees is unavailable:
 collection parameters or return types suggest iteration.
 Sets loopProperties (hasStreams, hasEnhancedFor, streamOps).

members.name: NameBehaviorGapChannel
members.description:
Channel 4: Name-Behavior Gap.
 Cross-references channel 1 (naming intent) and channel 2/3 (guard clauses/branches).
 If the name says "validate" but no guards are found, flags a gap.
 Does not require Trees (reads from already-populated signals).

members.name: NamingChannel
members.description:
Channel 1: Name Semantics.
 Uses NamingAnalyzer to extract verb, object, and intent category
 from the method name using camelCase conventions.

members.name: ReturnTypeChannel
members.description:
Channel 5: Return Type.
 Analyzes the method's return type for signals (Optional, List, void, etc.).
 Populates data flow reads/writes based on return type characteristics.

members.name: IntentDensityCalculator
members.description:
Calculates the Intent Signal Density (ISD) score for a method based on the collected intent signals across all 13 channels. The raw score ranges from 0.0 (no intent signals) to 1.0 (maximum intent density), and is then scaled to a 0.0–10.0 range for human readability.

ISD formula (weights sum to 1.0):

 ISD = w1*nameSemantics + w2*guardClauses + w3*branches + w4*dataFlow
     + w5*returnType + w6*loops + w7*errorHandling + w8*constants
     + w9*nullChecks + w10*assertions + w11*logging + w12*dependencies
     + w13*validationAnnotations

 Final score = min(10.0, ISD * 10.0)

methods.name: calculate
methods.description:
Calculates the ISD score for the given intent signals.
 The score is on a 0.0–10.0 scale where higher values indicate
 richer intent signal density.

params.name: signals
params.description: the collected intent signals for a method

examples.title: Compute ISD score from intent signals

members.name: IntentGraphExtractor
members.description:
Orchestrates DSTI (Documentation Semantic & Temporal Intelligence) analysis. For each method in a type, iterates through 13 independent IntentChannel implementations and merges their signals, then calculates the ISD score.

Each channel is self-contained and can be enabled/disabled individually for ablation studies. The orchestrator resolves the Trees API once and passes it to all channels that require AST access.

This extractor is only active when the DSTI config flag is enabled. It optionally uses {@code com.sun.source.util.Trees} for deeper AST analysis, but gracefully degrades if that API is unavailable.

methods.name: getChannels
methods.description:
Returns the immutable list of all 13 DSTI channels used by this extractor.
 Useful for ablation studies and channel-level diagnostics.

examples.title: Extract intent signals for a type

members.name: NamingAnalyzer
members.description:
Parses method names to extract semantic verbs and objects using camelCase conventions. Used by the DSTI (Documentation Semantic & Temporal Intelligence) system to infer developer intent from naming patterns.

Supports 150+ verb prefixes mapped to 10 intent categories. The verb dictionary is ordered longest-first to avoid prefix collisions (e.g., "initialize" before "init").

methods.name: analyze
methods.description:
Analyzes a method name and extracts its semantic components.

The algorithm tries verb-prefix matching first. For each known verb, it checks whether the method name starts with the verb followed by an uppercase letter (standard camelCase, e.g., "getUser") OR matches the verb exactly (single-word methods like "execute", "run", "close").

For two-letter verbs ("is", "of", "on", "do", "to"), we also accept the pattern verb + lowercase (e.g., "isEmpty" would not match normally because 'E' is uppercase, but "is" + "Empty" does match). Actually "isEmpty" does match because 'E' IS uppercase. The special case is for verbs where the remaining portion starts lowercase, like "isempty" — but that's non-standard Java.

params.name: methodName
params.description: the simple name of the method

returns.description: a NameSemantics record with verb, object, and intent category

examples.title: Extract verb and intent from method name

members.name: PropertyDSL
members.description:
Parses {@code @DocInvariant} rule expressions into structured InvariantRule objects.

Supported syntax:

  • {@code "field NOT_NULL"} — field must not be null
  • {@code "field SIZE > 0"} — collection/array size check
  • {@code "field IN [val1, val2, val3]"} — value must be one of the listed values
  • {@code "field BETWEEN 1 AND 100"} — numeric range check
  • {@code "field MATCHES pattern"} — regex pattern check
  • {@code "field > 0"}, {@code "field >= 0"}, {@code "field < 100"} — comparison
  • {@code "field == value"}, {@code "field != value"} — equality/inequality
  • {@code "field NOT_EMPTY"} — string/collection must not be empty
  • {@code "field NOT_BLANK"} — string must not be blank

methods.name: parse
methods.description: Parse a rule expression string into an InvariantRule.

returns.description: the parsed rule, or {@code null} if the expression cannot be parsed
list of successfully parsed rules (never null, may be empty)

members.name: ConfigurationExtractor
members.description:
Detects {@code @Value("${...}")} fields and {@code @ConfigurationProperties} classes,
 and populates the configuration section of the DocSpec model.

members.name: DatabaseSchemaIntrospector
members.description:
Introspects database schema metadata from JPA entity annotations to extract constraint details (check constraints, foreign keys, indexes, generation strategies, unique constraints, and enumerated types) that go beyond basic column mapping.

This extractor analyzes {@code @Table}, {@code @Index}, {@code @Column}, {@code @Id}, {@code @GeneratedValue}, {@code @JoinColumn}, {@code @UniqueConstraint}, {@code @SequenceGenerator}, {@code @TableGenerator}, {@code @Enumerated}, and relationship annotations ({@code @ManyToOne}, {@code @OneToMany}, {@code @OneToOne}, {@code @ManyToMany}) to populate the extended DataModel fields.

methods.name: compareWithLiveSchema
methods.description:
Compares JPA entity annotations against a live database schema accessed via JDBC. Returns a consistency report listing mismatches between the annotation-declared schema and the actual database structure.

This method connects to the specified database, reads its metadata through {@code java.sql.DatabaseMetaData}, and compares table names, column names, nullability, types, indexes, and foreign keys against what was extracted from annotations.

params.name: model
params.description: the DocSpec model containing annotation-extracted data models

returns.description: a list of mismatch descriptions; empty list means full consistency

members.name: DataStoreExtractor
members.description:
Detects JPA entities (tables), Spring Data repositories, and Redis/Kafka types,
 and populates the data stores section of the DocSpec model.

members.name: DocSpecExtractor
members.description:
Interface for extractors that analyze types and populate the DocSpec model
 with domain-specific documentation (security, configuration, observability, etc.).

methods.name: isAvailable
methods.description:
Checks whether this extractor can operate in the current environment.
 Typically verifies that required framework classes are on the classpath.

methods.name: extractorName
methods.description: Returns the human-readable name of this extractor for diagnostic messages.

methods.name: extract
methods.description: Analyzes a single type element and populates the model with extracted data.

members.name: ExternalDependencyExtractor
members.description:
Detects external service calls via {@code RestTemplate}, {@code WebClient},
 {@code RestClient}, and {@code @FeignClient} annotations, and populates the
 external dependencies section of the DocSpec model.

members.name: ObservabilityExtractor
members.description:
Detects Micrometer annotations ({@code @Timed}, {@code @Counted}) and
 Spring Actuator health indicators, populating the observability section
 of the DocSpec model.

members.name: OpenApiMerger
members.description:
Scans for OpenAPI specification files (openapi.json, openapi.yaml) on the classpath
 and merges endpoint descriptions into the DocSpec model.

methods.name: finalizeExtraction
methods.description:
Merge OpenAPI endpoint descriptions into the model.
 Called during processor finalization, not per-type.

members.name: PrivacyExtractor
members.description:
Detects {@code @DocPII} and {@code @DocSensitive} annotations on fields
 and populates the privacy section of the DocSpec model.

members.name: QueryIndexAnalyzer
members.description:
Analyzes Spring Data repository method names and {@code @Query} annotations to infer which database indexes are needed for efficient query execution.

For example, {@code findByEmailAndStatus(String, Status)} implies an index on {@code (email, status)} columns.

members.name: SchemaConsistencyChecker
members.description:
Compares JPA entity definitions against database schema metadata to detect inconsistencies such as missing columns, type mismatches, or constraint differences.

This runs as a post-processing step after all entities have been extracted, comparing the DataModel entries against known schema information.

methods.name: checkConsistency
methods.description:
Check schema consistency across all data models.
 Called during processor finalization.

members.name: SecurityExtractor
members.description:
Detects Spring Security annotations ({@code @PreAuthorize}, {@code @Secured},
 {@code @RolesAllowed}) on types and methods and populates the security section
 of the DocSpec model.

methods.name: applyMethodAnnotations
methods.description: Applies method-level Spring annotations (@Transactional, @Cacheable) to the method model.

methods.name: getClassBasePath
methods.description: Gets the class-level @RequestMapping base path for a controller.

methods.name: extractEndpointMapping
methods.description:
Extracts an EndpointMappingModel from Spring @GetMapping/@PostMapping/etc.
 Returns null if the method has no Spring mapping annotation.

methods.name: getRequestMappingPath
methods.description: Gets a request mapping path from the given method.

methods.name: getScheduleInfo
methods.description: Extracts cron or fixedRate info from @Scheduled annotation.

members.name: FlowStepDataStoreOpModel
members.description: flow step data store op model.

flows.name: DocSpec Processing Pipeline
flows.description: The 7-phase annotation processing pipeline that produces docspec.json with documentation and DSTI intent data. Triggered by mvn docspec:generate.

steps.name: Auto-Discovery
steps.description: Scans all public classes, detects Spring Boot/JPA/Jackson frameworks on classpath

steps.name: Read Metadata
steps.description: Reads @DocModule, @DocFlow, @DocMethod, JavaDoc comments. Infers descriptions from method names.

steps.name: Extract System Info
steps.description: Runs 7 extractors: Security, Config, Observability, DataStore, ExternalDeps, Privacy, ErrorsEvents

steps.name: DSTI Intent Extraction
steps.description: Runs 13 independent intent channels on every public method. Cross-verifies signals. Computes ISD scores.

steps.name: Cross-Reference Resolution
steps.description: Resolves @DocUses, builds error.thrownBy, dataModel.usedBy, member.referencedBy mappings

steps.name: Coverage Calculation
steps.description: Computes documentation coverage: % of classes documented, % of methods with descriptions

steps.name: Serialize Output
steps.description: Serializes the complete DocSpec model to target/docspec.json via Jackson ObjectMapper

flows.name: DSTI Intent Analysis Pipeline
flows.description: Analyzes every public method across 13 independent intent channels, cross-verifies signals, and computes an ISD score. Each channel is self-contained for ablation.
flows.trigger: DocSpecProcessor invokes extract() on a discovered type

steps.name: Resolve Trees API
steps.description: Obtains com.sun.source.util.Trees via reflection for AST-level analysis; gracefully degrades if unavailable

steps.name: Channel 1: Name Semantics
steps.description: Extracts verb, object, and intent category from method name via NamingAnalyzer

steps.name: Channel 2: Guard Clauses
steps.description: Counts if-throw guard patterns and @DocIntentional/@DocPreserves annotation-based guards

steps.name: Channel 3: Branch Structure
steps.description: Counts if-statements (branching complexity) from the AST

steps.name: Channel 4: Name-Behavior Gap
steps.description: Cross-references naming intent with guard/branch signals to detect naming inconsistencies

steps.name: Channel 5: Return Type
steps.description: Analyzes return type for Optional, Collection, reactive, void, or scalar signals

steps.name: Channel 6: Assignment Patterns
steps.description: Detects builder patterns, constructor calls, and data flow writes

steps.name: Channel 7: Loop Patterns
steps.description: Detects .stream(), enhanced-for loops, and specific stream operations (filter, map, flatMap)

steps.name: Channel 8: Error Handling
steps.description: Counts try/catch blocks and extracts caught exception types

steps.name: Channel 9: Constants
steps.description: Scans for UPPER_CASE constant references in the method body

steps.name: Channel 10: Null Checks
steps.description: Counts null check patterns: != null, == null, Objects.requireNonNull, Optional.ofNullable

steps.name: Channel 11: Assertions
steps.description: Detects assert statements and assertion-style method calls (check*, require*, verify*)

steps.name: Channel 12: Logging
steps.description: Detects logger method calls (log, debug, info, warn, error, trace)

steps.name: Channel 13: Validation Annotations
steps.description: Counts Bean Validation annotations on parameters (@NotNull, @Valid, @Min, @Max, @Size, etc.)

steps.name: Cross-Verification
steps.description: Collects injected field types as dependency signals for cross-cutting analysis

steps.name: ISD Score Calculation
steps.description: Computes weighted ISD score (0.0-1.0) from all 13 channel signals

crossRefs.description: Reads all 42 DocSpec annotations from source code to build the documentation model
Serializes the DocSpec model and intent graph to JSON output files
Delegates method name parsing to NamingAnalyzer for verb/object/intent extraction
Computes the weighted ISD score from merged channel signals

errors.description: Annotation processing failed due to an unrecoverable error during type analysis or model building.
errors.resolution: Check compiler diagnostics for the root cause. Ensure all dependencies are on the classpath.
causes: A type element could not be resolved during processing

errors.description: Failed to serialize the DocSpec model to JSON or write the output file.
errors.resolution: Check the docspec.output.dir configuration and ensure the target directory is writable.
causes: Output directory does not exist or is not writable
Jackson serialization encountered a cycle or unknown type
Disk is full or path is invalid

events.name: docspec.processing.started
events.description: Emitted when the DocSpec annotation processor begins its first processing round.
events.trigger: javac invocation with DocSpecProcessor on the annotation processor path

events.name: docspec.extraction.security
events.description: Emitted when Spring Security annotations have been extracted from a type.

events.name: docspec.serialization.completed
events.description: Emitted when docspec.json has been successfully written to the output directory.
